methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Archer Aviation is a publicly traded autonomous aircraft developer in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The company has raised over $1.5B and carries a current public market valuation of $5B. The relevant peer universe consists of six private companies operating in similar technology domains, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, Aurora Innovation, and ideaForge. No direct public comparables exist in this narrow subsector, limiting the application of traditional public market benchmarking.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only three companies meeting narrow peer criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier), falling below the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for statistically meaningful comparison. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneous business models, technology readiness levels, and commercialization timelines across broader autonomous systems categories. Consequently, no triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from the available comparable company data.

The analysis is fundamentally constrained by the absence of both public market benchmarks and a sufficiently dense private peer set. Valuation support would require either expansion of the comparable universe through adjacent subsectors (air mobility, autonomous logistics), application of DCF-based bottom-up methods tied to specific revenue milestones and unit economics, or additional private financing transactions establishing transaction-derived multiples. The current $5B public valuation should be assessed against Archer's capital deployed, path to certification, and competitive positioning rather than multiples-based inference.
